description: A well-qualified UK private dental practice prospect. Discovery call with clear problem, budget signals within range, single decision-maker present. Agent should produce a clean tiered proposal with the Growth tier recommended.

default_summary.markdown_formatted:
## Summary

Dr Priya Shah runs Meadow Lane Dental, a 3-chair private dental practice in Didsbury, Manchester. The practice does approximately £850k annual turnover, with 60% private 40% NHS mix but moving toward fully private.

**Key pain points:**
- Spending ~£2,400/month on Google Ads + Meta with what she calls 'terrible' ROI
- Only 32% of ad-driven phone enquiries convert to booked consultations
- Receptionist misses ~25 calls/week (Priya has checked the phone logs)
- No follow-up system for unconverted enquiries
- No content produced consistently — website blog last updated 2 years ago

**Budget signal:** mentioned they spent '£18k last year on marketing with what felt like no return'. Open to investing similarly or more if there's accountability.

**Timeline:** wants to see results before summer (June/July) — practice typically goes quieter in August.

**Decision:** Priya owns the practice outright. No other decision-maker.

**Competition:** evaluated one other agency 6 months ago, 'didn't like them, felt generic'. Hasn't shortlisted others.

**Tone/fit:** warm, fast-talking, asks good questions, wants specificity not fluff.

action_items.description: Send Priya a proposal covering: lead management for ad-driven enquiries, follow-up automation, and monthly content production
Reference a comparable dental practice case study if possible

transcript.text: Hi Priya, thanks for making time. Before we dive in — what made you reach out to us specifically?

Honestly, I saw your case study with the Edinburgh clinic. I'm spending about two and a half grand a month on Google Ads and Meta, and it feels like it's all going into a black hole. We're a 3-chair practice, Didsbury — I need these ads to actually convert and right now they don't.
Okay, that's helpful context. When you say it doesn't convert — what does that look like in practice? You're getting the enquiries, but they're not booking?

So I pulled the numbers before this call. We get roughly 80 enquiries a month from the ads. Of those, 26 actually book a consultation. So about 32% conversion. Which — that's terrible, right? And that's from the ones we even speak to. Laura at reception — she's brilliant but one person — she's missing about 25 calls a week. I've been through the phone logs. Twenty-five missed calls, every week.

And then the ones who don't book on the first call — nothing. We don't follow up. They're just gone. That's the one that kills me. I'm paying £30+ for each click and then when they don't book immediately we just... let them go.
Got it. So three distinct holes: missed calls, low first-call conversion, zero follow-up. What about content — blog, social, that side?

Oh god, don't. Our blog hasn't been updated in two years. I know I should. Every practice owner says that. I just don't have time to write, and I hate what writers produce when they're not me — it doesn't sound like me.
That's a really common one, and honestly one we solve well. Can I ask about budget? What were you spending before the ads started underperforming?

Last year total marketing spend was about 18 grand, and I can tell you almost none of that is attributable to new patient revenue. Which — the practice does about £850k a year, roughly 60/40 private to NHS, moving toward fully private. So the marketing should be earning its keep. I'd actually spend more if I could see it working. 2-3 grand a month plus ad spend, something in that range.
Understood. And timeline — when do you want to see movement on this?

Before summer. August is always quiet for us — holidays, schools — so I need the funnel working before then. Realistically, I'd want to see the missed-call and follow-up stuff fixed within 4 weeks. The content side I'm more patient with — 2-3 months is fine.
Who else is involved in this decision?
Just me. I own the practice outright. I'm the decision.
And have you looked at other providers?

I spoke to one about six months ago. Didn't feel right — they pitched me the same thing they pitch everyone, very generic. I haven't actively shopped since. You're really the only conversation I'm in.

Thank you, that's really helpful. Here's what I'd like to do: based on what you've shared, I'll pull together a proposal covering three things — the missed-call problem, the follow-up gap, and the content side. I'll have that to you within 48 hours. Sound good?
Yes please. And if you can include a case study from another dental practice — even just one — that would land really well with me. I trust results, not pitches.
Will do. Priya, thanks for your time. Expect the proposal by Friday end of day at the latest.
